title: lupinus pilosus subsp. cosentinii
intro: Lupinus pilosus subsp. cosentinii, also known as lupin, is a plant species belonging to the legume family and native to the Mediterranean region. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues, such as diarrhea and dyspepsia, due to its high saponin content. The health benefits of lupin include reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health, as well as its potential to aid in weight management and lower cholesterol levels. Lupin can be prepared as an herbal tea, dried capsule, or infused oil, and is also used as a food ingredient in some parts of the world, particularly in Italy, where it is valued for its nutritional value and unique flavor.
